## Acceptance Criteria

Pass. `jit graph rdeps` defaults to bounded depth 1 at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1478-1479`, and the top-level `jit rdeps` alias now also defaults to depth 1 at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160-163`. `--depth 0` remains the explicit unbounded opt-in through `show_rdeps_with_depth` at `crates/jit/src/commands/graph.rs:153-156`.

## Architecture And Separation Of Concerns

Pass. The CLI default and docs live in `cli.rs`; execution still routes through the canonical graph command in `main.rs:1017-1018`; traversal remains in `commands/graph.rs`. No new domain/storage/command/CLI boundary violation found.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. The new code is limited to CLI metadata and integration test coverage. Existing imperative BFS in `commands/graph.rs:165-188` is appropriate for graph traversal.

## Correctness And Invariants

Pass. The prior alias correctness issue is fixed: `jit rdeps <id>` now preserves the same bounded default as `jit graph rdeps <id>`. No new file writes, event logging paths, state transitions, assignee/label validation, PID handling, or unsafe code were introduced.

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code introduced. Workspace safety enforcement remains present via `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:14`.

## Testing

Pass. I did not rerun Cargo tests because the issue has a succeeded `cargo-ci` gate. Evidence: `.jit/gate-runs/313a9109-b420-479c-b289-b954c2c99c5c/result.json` shows commit `22cd3377`, exit code 0, with `fmt`, `clippy`, and `2790 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored`.

New regression coverage addresses the prior gap: `crates/jit/tests/first_guess_residuals_test.rs:213-260` verifies multi-level `jit rdeps` is bounded by default and `--depth 0` includes transitive dependents.

## Error Handling

Pass. No new production fallible operations or error types were added. New `unwrap()` usage is confined to tests.

## CLI Contract

Pass. `jit rdeps` retains `--json` at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:165-166` and normalizes to the canonical JSON-capable `GraphCommands::Rdeps` path in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1017-1018`.

## Documentation

Pass. Top-level alias docs now document depth 1 default and unbounded opt-in at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160-161`. Canonical `graph rdeps` docs also describe the bounded default and `--depth 0` opt-in at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1463-1468`.

## Prior Review Feedback

Pass. The most recent failed review found that the top-level alias still defaulted to unbounded depth and lacked multi-level alias coverage. Both are addressed by `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160-163` and `crates/jit/tests/first_guess_residuals_test.rs:213-260`.

## JIT Issue Management

Pass. Dependency `527bf0a6` is marked done in the provided issue context. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it exited 0 with `Repository validation passed`.

## Findings

Total findings: 0

VERDICT: PASS
